prompt: Build a RACI matrix for a mobile app development project with these roles: Product Owner, Scrum Master, Tech Lead, 3 Developers, QA Lead, UX Designer, and Project Sponsor. Key activities include requirements definition, sprint planning, development, code review, testing, deployment, and stakeholder demos.
assertions: Output assigns exactly one Accountable party per activity
Output includes all listed roles as columns and all listed activities as rows in the matrix
Output differentiates between R, A, C, and I assignments logically based on role responsibilities
Output flags or validates that no role is overloaded with disproportionate assignments

prompt: Validate this RACI matrix: Requirements — PM(A), BA(R), Dev(C); Design — UX(R,A), PM(C); Development — Dev(R), Tech Lead(A), PM(I); Testing — QA(R), Dev(C); Deployment — DevOps(R), PM(A), QA(C). Identify any structural issues.
assertions: Output identifies that UX having both R and A on Design violates the separation principle or flags it as a concern
Output checks for missing Informed assignments on activities where stakeholders should be notified
Output validates the single-A rule across all activities
Output assesses workload balance and flags any role with excessive assignments

prompt: A new data engineering team of 4 is joining the project in phase 2. Update the RACI matrix to incorporate their roles for data pipeline activities without disrupting existing assignments for the 8-person application team.
assertions: Output adds new activities specific to data engineering (pipeline development, data quality, ETL) to the matrix
Output assigns RACI roles to the new team members without modifying existing application team assignments
Output identifies handoff points between data engineering and application teams with appropriate C/I assignments
Output validates the expanded matrix for conflicts or gaps at the integration boundaries

prompt: The project has a vendor team responsible for API integration. The vendor reports to their own management but the project PM needs visibility. Design the RACI assignments that properly handle the vendor boundary.
assertions: Output assigns the vendor team as Responsible for API integration work without making them Accountable for internal project decisions
Output ensures internal project roles retain Accountability for deliverables that depend on vendor work
Output includes Consulted or Informed assignments that give the PM appropriate visibility into vendor activities
Output addresses the dual-reporting dynamic with clear escalation or coordination assignments
